commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f8233cff20c5b4a8dabb79d6e97f33583c0a11a8)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds subagent registry deadline handling for explicit `runTimeoutSeconds` plain `agent.wait` timeouts and adds a regression test for that case.

PR surface: Source +27, Tests +59. Total +86 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, not from a live run in this review. Current main retries forever when `agent.wait` returns a plain timeout and the child session row still says running, while the linked report gives concrete stalled subagent examples and a repro sketch.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor real behavior proof or maintainer override before merge; I found no narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to attempt.

target.title: [codex] fix subagent run timeout deadline
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87444.

- Enforce explicit `runTimeoutSeconds` as the terminal deadline for subagent runs when `agent.wait` returns a plain timeout without terminal snapshot fields.
- Complete the child run with a timeout outcome at the stored deadline so parent wakeup, final delivery, and cleanup can proceed instead of retrying forever.
- Add a regression test for the reported shape: gateway wait timeout, running child session row, and elapsed explicit run timeout.

## Root Cause

The subagent registry treated a plain `agent.wait` timeout as non-terminal unless gateway also returned terminal fields such as `endedAt`, `stopReason`, or `livenessState`, or the session store had already settled. When `sessions_spawn` supplied `runTimeoutSeconds` but gateway timed out without a terminal snapshot, the registry scheduled another wait retry and left the child active indefinitely.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1 HEAD`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` child runs with explicit `runTimeoutSeconds` no longer remain active after the deadline when `agent.wait` only reports a non-terminal timeout.
Real environment tested: local Codex worktree with the repo Vitest wrapper; no live channel/provider run.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the three focused Vitest targets listed above after rebasing the branch onto `origin/main`, plus `git diff --check HEAD~1 HEAD`.
Evidence after fix: the new regression in `src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts` proves the child stays active before the deadline, then records `status: "timeout"`, `endedAt` at the exact deadline, and runs the announce path once after the retry.
Observed result after fix: all three focused test targets passed on the final commit.
What was not tested: live end-to-end channel/provider behavior and GitHub CI have not run yet.
target.author: monkeyleet
